message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

Mount ₢A-ABH (caparison-windows-decompose) under ₣A-. Docket carries
  four locked decisions from the prior chat — read via gazette after
  jjx_orient.

  Field state on bujn-winpc: sshd_config reverted to clean post-directive-
  merge content (sshd -t green; running sshd still on OLD config in
  memory). rbtww-main WSL distribution unregistered; C:\WSL swept. New
  C:\bujb-wsl directory not yet provisioned — caparison Phase 3 will land
  it. ₢A-ABG (windows-setup-first-time-debug) remains open behind ₢A-ABH;
  three notches landed there already, most recent 9dc7fd02 minting
  WSp-109 (helper escape invariant) + three invigilate-windows caparison
  facts (Match block / authkeys dir presence / authkeys dir ACL) + the
  corresponding BUSJIW spec additions. Those underlie ₢A-ABH's call
  surface.

  Scope is mechanical decomposition per the docket. No new design surface.

  Reading order: docket (gazette), WSG-WindowsScriptingGuide.md
  (especially WSp-105 and the memo reference at its tail), BUSJCW
  (current Phase 1 — to be rewritten as 13 axhos_steps), BUSJGW
  (orchestration-style exemplar to mirror), bujb_jurisdiction.sh
  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1 + the two exec helpers
  (_with_password_fallback at ~1413, _keyonly at ~1437) + the
  BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin constant (line ~212) — all targeted for
  deletion or restructure.

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
8	WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
9	integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
10	WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.
11	
12	## Current Concept
13	
14	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
15	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
16	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
17	Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
18	in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
19	
20	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
21	
22	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
23	  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
24	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
25	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
26	  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
27	  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
28	  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
29	  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
30	  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.
31	
32	**BUJB constant:**
33	
34	- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
35	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
36	  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.
37	
38	**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**
39	
40	- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
41	  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
42	  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
43	  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
44	  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
45	  admin-shell operations.
46	- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
47	  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
48	  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
49	  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
50	  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
51	  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
52	  precondition check.
53	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
54	  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
55	  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
56	  authorized_keys.
57	
58	**Garrison-destructive model:**
59	
60	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
61	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
62	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
63	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
64	- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
65	  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.
66	
67	**Cross-cutting premises:**
68	
69	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
70	  it by reference, never duplicates.
71	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
72	  material; operator owns all key administration.
73	
74	**Tabtarget colophons (20 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
75	
76	Invigilate has no tabtarget — it is invoked only internally (caparison
77	post-completion, garrison precondition via bujp_preflight). The
78	`bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}` functions exist; no standalone
79	operator invocation.
80	
81	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
82	- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
83	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
84	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
85	- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
86	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
87	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
88	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
89	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
90	- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3
91	
92	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
93	
94	BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
95	implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
96	mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
97	privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
98	distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
99	hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
100	live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.
101	
102	**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**
103	
104	Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
105	admin posture, then post-completion verification.
106	
107	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
108	
109	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
110	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
111	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
112	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
113	   process never sees the password.
114	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
115	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
116	   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
117	   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
118	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
119	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
120	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
121	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
122	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
123	   values.
124	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
125	   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
126	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
127	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
128	   expected phase boundary).
129	
130	Phase 2 (key auth only):
131	
132	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
133	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
134	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
135	   a session.
136	9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
137	
138	Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):
139	
140	10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
141	    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
142	11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
143	12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
144	    (idempotent).
145	13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
146	    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
147	    wrong.
148	
149	**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**
150	
151	Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
152	ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
153	auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.
154	
155	**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**
156	
157	Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
158	systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
159	invigilate-linux post-check.
160	
161	**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**
162	
163	Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
164	fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
165	and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
166	posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
167	remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
168	garrison's precondition check.
169	
170	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
171	
172	1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
173	   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
174	2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
175	   only (no password fallback).
176	3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
177	   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
178	   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
179	4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
180	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
181	5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
182	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
183	6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
184	   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
185	   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
186	7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
187	
188	## Heat Sequence
189	
190	**Spec foundation:**
191	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
192	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
193	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
194	  caparison)
195	
196	**Implementation:**
197	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
198	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
199	  superseded colophons + handbook entries
200	
201	**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
202	- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
203	  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
204	- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
205	  then macOS/Linux)
206	- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
207	  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)
208	
209	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
210	- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
211	
212	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
213	- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
214	- Garrison-WSL exercise
215	- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
216	- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)
217	
218	## Deferred (named for future return)
219	
220	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
221	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
222	  only.
223	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
224	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
225	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
226	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
227	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
228	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
229	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
230	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
231	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
232	- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
233	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
234	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
235	  caparison phase or sibling verb.
236	- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
237	  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
238	  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
239	  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
240	  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
241	  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
242	  a time, never both running concurrently).
243	- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
244	  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.
245	
246	## Standing Notes
247	
248	- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
249	  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
250	  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
251	  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
252	  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
253	  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
254	  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
255	  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
256	  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
257	  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
258	  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
259	- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
260	  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
261	  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
262	  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
263	  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
264	- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
265	  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
266	  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
267	  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
268	  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
269	  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
270	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
271	  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
272	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
273	  privkey-at-rest protection.
274	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
275	  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
276	  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
277	  precondition.
278	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
279	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
280	  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
281	  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
282	- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
283	  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
284	  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
285	  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
286	  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
287	  daemon ever runs concurrently.
288	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
289	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
290	  and path construction.
291	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
292	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
293	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
294	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
295	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
296	  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
297	- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
298	  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
299	  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
300	  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
301	- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
302	  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
303	- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
304	  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
305	  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
306	  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
307	  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
308	  is preferred for shared hosts.
309	- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
310	  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
311	  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.
312	
313	# jjezs_pace ₢A-ABH
314	
315	## Character
316	Mechanical decomposition guided by ₢A-ABG chat decisions: helper layer
317	deletions, Phase 1 callsite restructure into single-cmdlet steps, BUSJCW
318	spec rewrite as orchestration-style exemplar, field re-test on bujn-winpc.
319	No new design surface — implementation against locked decisions.
320	
321	## Goal
322	Caparison-windows Phase 1 runs as ~13 single-cmdlet steps per WSp-105.
323	The `powershell -NoProfile -File -` heredoc-fed invocation pattern leaves
324	caparison entirely. Password-fallback transport bounded to one operation
325	(admin pubkey install via single `Add-Content`) and gated by a key-auth
326	CDD probe — first-caparison-ever on a host prompts once for password;
327	every re-run is key-only end-to-end. BUSJCW Phase 1 follows the
328	orchestration-style discipline minted by the BUSJGW restructure
329	(461c65aa) with 13 axhos_steps; "chunk A / chunk B" vocabulary retires.
330	
331	## Locked decisions (from ₢A-ABG chat)
332	- Password-fallback session = single `Add-Content` cmdlet. No existing
333	  WSp exemption covers bounded compound bodies; simple form chosen.
334	- Key-auth probe at caparison start (`zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0'`);
335	  skip the password-fallback session when key auth already works.
336	- Restart-Service is key-only — admin pubkey installed by that point;
337	  password-fallback at restart guards an impossible failure mode.
338	- 13 axhos_steps in BUSJCW Phase 1; no grouping.
339	- Delete: `zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback`,
340	  `zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly`, `BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin`.
341	
342	## Done when
343	Caparison-windows on bujn-winpc converges with the decomposed shape (at
344	most one password prompt on a fresh host, zero on re-runs). Spec and
345	bash coherent. Gates green (bash -n, buw-st, handbook-render, fast).
346	Garrison-w then re-runs cleanly against the post-caparison posture as
347	the gate back to ₢A-ABG's iterative debug.

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.

**BUJB constant:**

- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.

**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**

- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
  admin-shell operations.
- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
  precondition check.
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (20 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

Invigilate has no tabtarget — it is invoked only internally (caparison
post-completion, garrison precondition via bujp_preflight). The
`bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}` functions exist; no standalone
operator invocation.

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.

**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**

Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
admin posture, then post-completion verification.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values.
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.

Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):

10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
    (idempotent).
13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
    wrong.

**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**

Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.

**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**

Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
invigilate-linux post-check.

**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**

Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
garrison's precondition check.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback).
3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
  caparison)

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
  superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
  then macOS/Linux)
- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
- Garrison-WSL exercise
- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  caparison phase or sibling verb.
- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
  a time, never both running concurrently).
- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.

## Standing Notes

- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
  precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
  daemon ever runs concurrently.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
  and path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
  is preferred for shared hosts.
- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-ABH

## Character
Mechanical decomposition guided by ₢A-ABG chat decisions: helper layer
deletions, Phase 1 callsite restructure into single-cmdlet steps, BUSJCW
spec rewrite as orchestration-style exemplar, field re-test on bujn-winpc.
No new design surface — implementation against locked decisions.

## Goal
Caparison-windows Phase 1 runs as ~13 single-cmdlet steps per WSp-105.
The `powershell -NoProfile -File -` heredoc-fed invocation pattern leaves
caparison entirely. Password-fallback transport bounded to one operation
(admin pubkey install via single `Add-Content`) and gated by a key-auth
CDD probe — first-caparison-ever on a host prompts once for password;
every re-run is key-only end-to-end. BUSJCW Phase 1 follows the
orchestration-style discipline minted by the BUSJGW restructure
(461c65aa) with 13 axhos_steps; "chunk A / chunk B" vocabulary retires.

## Locked decisions (from ₢A-ABG chat)
- Password-fallback session = single `Add-Content` cmdlet. No existing
  WSp exemption covers bounded compound bodies; simple form chosen.
- Key-auth probe at caparison start (`zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0'`);
  skip the password-fallback session when key auth already works.
- Restart-Service is key-only — admin pubkey installed by that point;
  password-fallback at restart guards an impossible failure mode.
- 13 axhos_steps in BUSJCW Phase 1; no grouping.
- Delete: `zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback`,
  `zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly`, `BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin`.

## Done when
Caparison-windows on bujn-winpc converges with the decomposed shape (at
most one password prompt on a fresh host, zero on re-runs). Spec and
bash coherent. Gates green (bash -n, buw-st, handbook-render, fast).
Garrison-w then re-runs cleanly against the post-caparison posture as
the gate back to ₢A-ABG's iterative debug.

lastPrompt: Mount ₢A-ABH (caparison-windows-decompose) under ₣A-. Docket carries   four locked decisions from the prior chat — read via gazette after   jjx_orient.    Field state on bujn-winpc: sshd_config revert…

118	on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows
119	
120	//axhos_step
121	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
122	.. {busc_call}
123	`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
124	.. {busc_store}
125	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
126	.. {busc_require}
127	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
128	.. {busc_fatal}
129	on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
133	.. {busc_call}
134	`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
135	of phase 1
136	.. {busc_fatal}
137	on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
138	expected phase boundary)
139	
140	//axhos_step
141	. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
142	.. {busc_call}
143	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
144	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
145	.. {busc_store}
146	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
147	.. {busc_fatal}
148	on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
149	session); recover via operator's local console
150	
151	//axhos_step
152	. *Preflight — verify operator-precondition facts before phase 3*:
153	.. {busc_call}
154	{bust_invigilate_windows} operator-precondition fact subset
155	(`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` registry, `PlatformAoAcOverride`
156	registry, Tailscale service registered) over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`
157	.. {busc_store}
158	`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»`
159	.. {busc_require}
160	`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»` is zero
161	.. {busc_fatal}
162	on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope; preflight fails fast
163	before the multi-minute WSL stage so missing operator posture does
164	not waste a download cycle
165	
166	//axhos_step
167	. *Phase 3 — provision C:\\bujb-wsl directory*:
168	.. {busc_call}
169	`New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'C:\\bujb-wsl' -Force` over
170	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` -- idempotent. Holds admin's `rbtww-main`
171	distribution VHD tree AND serves as the admin↔workload handoff
172	point for the seed tarball that {busn_garrison} (WSL letter) writes
173	during step 3. Default ACL inheritance from `C:\\` grants
174	`BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`, which is what allows the workload
175	SSH session in garrison-w step 11 to read the seed -- the seed must
176	NOT live under workload's Windows profile because admin's
177	`wsl --export` writes file ACLs that exclude the workload user
178	.. {busc_fatal}
179	on directory-creation failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
180	
181	//axhos_step
182	. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
183	.. {busc_call}
184	`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
185	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
186	at `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main` (canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution`
187	in {bujb_jurisdiction})
188	.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
189	.. {busc_fatal}
190	on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
191	admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted
192	
193	//axhos_step
194	. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
195	.. {busc_call}
196	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
197	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
198	`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
199	.. {busc_fatal}
200	on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
201	Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)
202	
203	//axhos_step
204	. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
205	.. {busc_call}
206	`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
207	`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
208	.. {busc_fatal}
209	on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
210	{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)
211	
212	//axhos_step
213	. *Post-completion — verify caparison deliverables*:
214	.. {busc_call}
215	{bust_invigilate_windows} caparison-deliverable fact subset
216	(Tailscale service `Automatic`, standby-timeout policy = 0 AC+DC,
217	hibernate disabled, `rbtww-main` WSL distribution registered) over
218	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`. Operator-precondition facts already verified
219	at preflight do not re-run, and admin SSH is not re-probed because
220	phase 2 just established it
221	.. {busc_store}
222	`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»`
223	.. {busc_require}
224	`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»` is zero
225	.. {busc_fatal}
226	on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim
227	
228	//axhog_guarantee
229	On success:
230	
231	* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
232	  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
233	* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
234	  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`, AND a
235	  `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block routing
236	  `AuthorizedKeysFile` to the admin-owned absolute path
237	  `__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`.
238	* `__PROGRAMDATA__\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}\\` directory
239	  exists with admins/SYSTEM FullControl, inheritance disabled, no
240	  workload grant -- sshd reads as SYSTEM, ready for {busn_garrison}
241	  to write the trust file content (the file itself is garrison's
242	  responsibility, not caparison's).
243	* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
244	* `C:\\bujb-wsl\\` directory exists (admin↔workload handoff point;
245	  default-inherited ACL grants workload `BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`).
246	* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user
247	  at `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main`.
248	* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
249	* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
250	* {bust_invigilate_windows}'s caparison-deliverable fact subset
251	  confirms each post-trust posture fact (operator-precondition facts
252	  were verified at the phase 2/3 preflight gate); a standalone
253	  {bust_invigilate_windows} run after caparison succeeds asserts the
254	  full fact list end-to-end.
255	
256	//axhoc_completion
257	Exit 0 when the post-completion deliverables-subset
258	{bust_invigilate_windows} passes. Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure
259	(admin SSH, pubkey, ACL, `sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect
260	failure, preflight failure (operator-precondition facts), any phase-3
261	op failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
262	deliverables-subset failure.

//axhob_operation
{bust_caparison_windows}

NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
key trust + `sshd_config` hardening. Phase 2: key-only reconnect.
Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL distribution, powercfg,
Tailscale service). Closes with {bust_invigilate_windows}.

NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
the idempotent admin-shell remainder.

NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
failure. PowerShell-side operations use `Get-Content`, `Set-Service`,
`Start-Service`, `Restart-Service`, `wsl.exe --import`; bash-side
BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config` directives
from the raw bytes.

The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
Caparison presupposes this scope.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH session*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (steady-state: key via
{burp_privileged_key_file}; first run: TTY password)
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` for phase 1 operations
.. {busc_fatal}
on connection failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — derive admin pubkey*:
.. {busc_call}
`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file}
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
.. {busc_fatal}
on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — install admin pubkey*:
.. write `«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` to `administrators_authorized_keys` over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` as a bare key entry (no `command=`)
.. nuclear -- file replaced, not merged
.. {busc_fatal}
on write failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
.. {busc_call}
`icacls` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- ACL restricted to SYSTEM and
Administrators (Windows OpenSSH refuses the file otherwise);
idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `icacls` failure -- phase 2 reconnect would fail

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — write hardened sshd_config directives*:
.. write `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`,
`PermitEmptyPasswords no` to `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`;
idempotent
.. ensure `sshd_config` carries a `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}`
block routing `AuthorizedKeysFile` to
`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys` --
absolute path decouples sshd's workload authorized_keys lookup from
workload's `$HOME` (so a future profile demotion cannot lock out
auth); parallel to the built-in `Match Group administrators` block
routing to `administrators_authorized_keys`. Idempotent
.. provision the parent directory
`__PROGRAMDATA__\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}\\` over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` with ACL: admins/SYSTEM Full Control,
inheritance disabled, no workload grant -- sshd reads as
`NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM` and accesses the trust file under its own
identity, parallel to `administrators_authorized_keys` ACL
discipline. Idempotent. The directory persists across
{busn_garrison} destroy-and-recreate cycles -- garrison rewrites
the file contents but never touches the directory
.. {busc_fatal}
on write or ACL failure -- restart would still serve unhardened auth
or workload auth path would not resolve

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — verify hardened directives took*:
.. {busc_call}
PowerShell `Get-Content` on `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
directive values (BCG-compliant matching), AND contains a
`Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block whose `AuthorizedKeysFile`
resolves to
`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
.. {busc_fatal}
on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
.. {busc_call}
`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
of phase 1
.. {busc_fatal}
on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
expected phase boundary)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
.. {busc_fatal}
on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
session); recover via operator's local console

//axhos_step
. *Preflight — verify operator-precondition facts before phase 3*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} operator-precondition fact subset
(`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` registry, `PlatformAoAcOverride`
registry, Tailscale service registered) over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`
.. {busc_store}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope; preflight fails fast
before the multi-minute WSL stage so missing operator posture does
not waste a download cycle

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — provision C:\\bujb-wsl directory*:
.. {busc_call}
`New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'C:\\bujb-wsl' -Force` over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` -- idempotent. Holds admin's `rbtww-main`
distribution VHD tree AND serves as the admin↔workload handoff
point for the seed tarball that {busn_garrison} (WSL letter) writes
during step 3. Default ACL inheritance from `C:\\` grants
`BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`, which is what allows the workload
SSH session in garrison-w step 11 to read the seed -- the seed must
NOT live under workload's Windows profile because admin's
`wsl --export` writes file ACLs that exclude the workload user
.. {busc_fatal}
on directory-creation failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
.. {busc_call}
`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
at `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main` (canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution`
in {bujb_jurisdiction})
.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
.. {busc_fatal}
on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
.. {busc_call}
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
.. {busc_call}
`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)

//axhos_step
. *Post-completion — verify caparison deliverables*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} caparison-deliverable fact subset
(Tailscale service `Automatic`, standby-timeout policy = 0 AC+DC,
hibernate disabled, `rbtww-main` WSL distribution registered) over
`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`. Operator-precondition facts already verified
at preflight do not re-run, and admin SSH is not re-probed because
phase 2 just established it
.. {busc_store}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim

//axhog_guarantee
On success:

* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`, AND a
  `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block routing
  `AuthorizedKeysFile` to the admin-owned absolute path
  `__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`.
* `__PROGRAMDATA__\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}\\` directory
  exists with admins/SYSTEM FullControl, inheritance disabled, no
  workload grant -- sshd reads as SYSTEM, ready for {busn_garrison}
  to write the trust file content (the file itself is garrison's
  responsibility, not caparison's).
* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
* `C:\\bujb-wsl\\` directory exists (admin↔workload handoff point;
  default-inherited ACL grants workload `BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`).
* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user
  at `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main`.
* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
* {bust_invigilate_windows}'s caparison-deliverable fact subset
  confirms each post-trust posture fact (operator-precondition facts
  were verified at the phase 2/3 preflight gate); a standalone
  {bust_invigilate_windows} run after caparison succeeds asserts the
  full fact list end-to-end.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the post-completion deliverables-subset
{bust_invigilate_windows} passes. Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure
(admin SSH, pubkey, ACL, `sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect
failure, preflight failure (operator-precondition facts), any phase-3
op failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
deliverables-subset failure.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_wsl}
3	
4	NOTE: Workload identity inhabits *three namespaces*: a Windows local
5	user (auth boundary discovered by Windows OpenSSH), the workload's
6	*own* registered WSL distribution `rbtww-main` (populated in
7	workload's `HKCU\Lxss` via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball), and a
8	Linux user inside that workload-owned distribution (execution context
9	entered by the locked-down `command=` directive's `wsl.exe --user`).
10	The third namespace is forced by Microsoft's per-user WSL design --
11	distributions register only in the running user's `HKCU\Lxss`;
12	admin's distributions are invisible to workload sessions.
13	
14	NOTE: Auth is decoupled from workload-profile state via an
15	admin-owned absolute-path authorized_keys.
16	{busn_caparison}
17	installs `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` in `sshd_config` routing
18	`AuthorizedKeysFile` to
19	`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`.
20	Sshd's trust-file lookup never resolves the workload's `$HOME`.
21	
22	NOTE: Workload provisioning runs as *four* atomic SSH-as-workload
23	sessions (step 11), each carrying a single cmdlet or native-binary
24	call per WSG WSp-105. The fourth session is `wsl.exe --shutdown`
25	alone, releasing the `NTUSER.DAT` hive before Windows unloads that
26	logon. Best-effort minimum-session-count: WSG-strict single-cmdlet
27	bodies and single-session-with-shutdown are mutually exclusive in
28	the transport stack, so multi-session demotion risk during sessions
29	1-3 is accepted; Layer 1 (caparison-installed absolute-path Match
30	block + admin-owned authorized_keys) is the auth defense if
31	demotion occurs. See Rationale.
32	
33	NOTE: Cleanup (steps 4-8) is best-effort. Windows User Profile
34	Service performs asynchronous internal bookkeeping after profile
35	removal, so a single-pass 100% pristine state is not always
36	achievable; discipline is sufficient sweep that step 9's
37	`New-LocalUser` lands on a clean SID with the canonical profile
38	path.
39	
40	NOTE: Garrison-w does not self-validate the locked-down round-trip.
41	The operator's first workload-SSH invocation post-garrison
42	(`buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`) IS the round-trip validation; an
43	explicit invigilate fact for this assertion was retired as
44	overdesign once those operational tabtargets existed.
45	
46	The
47	{busn_garrison}
48	operation on Windows via WSL:
49	
50	//axhopt_typed_parameter
51	{burp_investiture}
52	
53	//axhoq_precondition
54	A
55	{burp_regime}
56	profile exists at the given
57	{burp_investiture}
58	and its
59	{burn_regime}
60	targets a Windows OpenSSH node, reachable as
61	{burp_privileged_user}
62	under key-only auth using
63	{burp_privileged_key_file}.
64	
65	//axhoq_precondition
66	{busn_caparison}
67	has run for this
68	{burp_investiture}
69	-- admin SSH trust established, `rbtww-main` staged under the admin
70	user at `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main`, sshd_config carries the `Match
71	User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block routing `AuthorizedKeysFile` to
72	`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
73	(parent directory pre-provisioned with admins/SYSTEM FullControl,
74	no workload grant -- sshd reads as SYSTEM, parallel to
75	`administrators_authorized_keys`).
76	
77	//axhoq_precondition
78	{burp_workload_key_file}
79	is unencrypted and exists on the operator's station.
80	
81	//axhos_step
82	. *Assert admin posture*:
83	.. {busc_call}
84	{bust_invigilate_windows}
85	for this
86	{burp_investiture}
87	.. {busc_store}
88	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
89	.. {busc_require}
90	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
91	.. {busc_fatal}
92	on non-zero --
93	{bust_handbook_windows}
94	scope (operator-managed) or
95	{bust_caparison_windows}
96	scope (caparison-managed); diagnostic surfaced verbatim
97	
98	//axhos_step
99	. *Open admin SSH session*:
100	.. {busc_call}
101	ssh as
102	{burp_privileged_user}
103	with
104	{burp_privileged_key_file};
105	key-auth only
106	.. {busc_store}
107	`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
108	.. {busc_fatal}
109	on connection failure --
110	{bust_caparison_windows}
111	scope
112	
113	//axhos_step
114	. *Export admin's rbtww-main as workload-readable seed*:
115	.. {busc_call}
116	`wsl.exe --export rbtww-main C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-seed.tar` over
117	`«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- writes the seed at the
118	{busn_caparison}-provisioned `C:\\bujb-wsl\\` directory whose default
119	inheritance from `C:\\` grants `BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`,
120	making the seed readable by the workload SSH session in step 11.
121	Writing under the workload's Windows profile fails because admin's
122	`wsl --export` inherits admin's logon-SID ACL on the resulting file,
123	excluding the workload user
124	.. {busc_store}
125	`«SEED_TARBALL»` = `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-seed.tar`
126	.. {busc_fatal}
127	on export failure --
128	{bust_caparison_windows}
129	scope
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Obliterate Phase 1 -- Release hives*:
133	.. {busc_call}
134	`wsl.exe --shutdown` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- host-wide WSL VM
135	teardown
136	.. {busc_call}
137	`Stop-Process -Name wslhost,wslservice -Force -ErrorAction
138	SilentlyContinue` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- releases helper
139	processes that survive shutdown
140	.. {busc_fatal}
141	on either call's failure --
142	{bust_handbook_windows}
143	scope
144	
145	//axhos_step
146	. *Obliterate Phase 2 -- Profile destruction via Win32_UserProfile*:
147	.. {busc_call}
148	`Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_UserProfile -Filter "LocalPath =
149	'C:\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}' OR LocalPath LIKE
150	'C:\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}.%'"` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` --
151	exact-match for canonical path + LIKE for numbered fallback dirs
152	from prior demotions; the unbounded `<user>%` form is forbidden
153	because it would prefix-match unrelated accounts like
154	`${BUJB_workload_user}_alt`. Emits one row per matching profile
155	.. For each row, {busc_call}
156	`Remove-CimInstance -Query "SELECT * FROM Win32_UserProfile WHERE
157	LocalPath = '<row.LocalPath>'"` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- one SSH
158	call per row per WSG WSp-105; User Profile Service handles
159	ProfileList SID key removal (canonical AND `.bak` variants),
160	`NTUSER.DAT` hive unload, and folder deletion atomically
161	.. {busc_fatal}
162	on probe or per-row remove failure --
163	{bust_handbook_windows}
164	scope
165	
166	//axhos_step
167	. *Obliterate Phase 3 -- SAM scrub*:
168	.. {busc_call}
169	`Remove-LocalUser -Name '${BUJB_workload_user}' -ErrorAction
170	SilentlyContinue` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- collapsed dispatch per
171	the WSG WSp-108 nuclear-cleanup carve-out: narrow failure spectrum
172	at this callsite (admin context, SAM database, fixed workload user
173	name); step 9's `New-LocalUser` provides the downstream verification
174	by erroring on "account already exists" if a silent partial cleanup
175	left a SID behind
176	.. {busc_fatal}
177	on SSH transport failure --
178	{bust_handbook_windows}
179	scope
180	
181	//axhos_step
182	. *Obliterate Phase 4 -- Filesystem sweep*:
183	.. {busc_call}
184	`Get-ChildItem -Path 'C:\\Users' -Filter '${BUJB_workload_user}*'
185	-Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` --
186	dynamic discovery; emits zero or more directory rows that survived
187	Phase 2 (canonical and any numbered fallback dirs)
188	.. For each row, {busc_call}
189	`Remove-Item -Recurse -Force -LiteralPath '<row.FullName>'` over
190	`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
191	.. {busc_call}
192	`Test-Path 'C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${BUJB_workload_user}'` over
193	`«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- non-destructive probe; returns state
194	.. {busc_store}
195	`«CYGWIN_HOME_PRESENT»` (`True` or `False`)
196	.. On `«CYGWIN_HOME_PRESENT»` equal to `True`, {busc_call}
197	`Remove-Item -Recurse -Force
198	'C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${BUJB_workload_user}'` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
199	-- probe-then-act per WSp-108 main rule; the nuclear-cleanup
200	carve-out does not qualify for arbitrary filesystem paths (NTFS ACL
201	friction, junction loops, file-locked-by-process expand the failure
202	spectrum beyond the carve-out's narrow-spectrum condition)
203	.. {busc_fatal}
204	on Windows-side probe or per-row remove failure --
205	{bust_handbook_windows}
206	scope
207	
208	//axhos_step
209	. *Obliterate Phase 5 -- WSL vestige*:
210	.. {busc_call}
211	`wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root userdel
212	${BUJB_workload_user}` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- absent-state
213	(userdel exit code 6) is acceptable and treated as no-op; user
214	lives inside admin's `rbtww-main` as vestige from prior garrison
215	shapes
216	.. {busc_call}
217	`wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root rm -rf
218	/home/${BUJB_workload_user}` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- always-run
219	cleanup of orphan home from any prior partial garrison
220	.. {busc_fatal}
221	on userdel exit code other than 0 or 6, or on `rm -rf` failure --
222	{bust_handbook_windows}
223	scope
224	
225	//axhos_step
226	. *Create Windows workload account*:
227	.. {busc_call}
228	`net.exe user /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes
229	${BUJB_workload_user}` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- creates
230	{bujb_workload_user}
231	in the SAM database
232	(`HKLM\\SAM\\SAM\\Domains\\Account\\Users`). The
233	`HKLM\\...\\ProfileList\\<SID>` entry is *not* written by
234	`net.exe user /add`; User Profile Service populates it on the first
235	interactive logon, which the step 11 workload SSH session triggers
236	via `wsl.exe --import` and the surrounding logon ceremony
237	.. No Linux user is created inside admin's `rbtww-main` -- the
238	workload's Linux user is minted inside the workload's *own*
239	imported distribution in step 11
240	.. {busc_fatal}
241	on account-management failure
242	
243	//axhos_step
244	. *Place bare workload trust*:
245	.. {busc_call}
246	`ssh-keygen -y` on
247	{burp_workload_key_file}
248	.. {busc_store}
249	`«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` from stdout
250	.. {busc_call}
251	`Set-Content` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` writing `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` as
252	a bare entry (no `command=` directive) to
253	`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
254	-- single cmdlet per WSG WSp-105; admin-owned file throughout its
255	lifetime; directory pre-provisioned by
256	{busn_caparison}
257	with admins/SYSTEM FullControl only (sshd reads as SYSTEM, parallel
258	to `administrators_authorized_keys`)
259	.. {busc_fatal}
260	on key-write failure
261	
262	//axhos_step
263	. *Provision workload's WSL (four atomic SSH-as-workload sessions per WSG WSp-105)*:
264	.. {busc_call}
265	*Session 1 (import)*: ssh as
266	{bujb_workload_user}
267	with
268	{burp_workload_key_file}
269	running `wsl.exe --import rbtww-main <workload-wsl-root>
270	«SEED_TARBALL»` -- registers the workload's own `rbtww-main` in
271	workload's `HKCU\\Lxss`; `<workload-wsl-root>` is a workload-writable
272	directory inside the workload's Windows profile. The bare entry at
273	the absolute-path authorized_keys authenticates this session
274	.. {busc_call}
275	*Session 2 (useradd)*: ssh as
276	{bujb_workload_user}
277	running `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root useradd
278	--create-home --shell /bin/bash ${BUJB_workload_user}` -- mints the
279	inner Linux workload user. `useradd` produces a locked-by-default
280	account per `/etc/shadow` convention (shadow entry `!` when `-p`
281	is not supplied); no separate `passwd --lock` session required
282	.. {busc_call}
283	*Session 3 (privkey)*: ssh as
284	{bujb_workload_user}
285	running `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root install -D
286	-m 600 -o ${BUJB_workload_user} -g ${BUJB_workload_user} /dev/stdin
287	/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519`, redirecting
288	{burp_workload_key_file}
289	to ssh's stdin -- the key flows through ssh FD 0 -> cmd.exe -> wsl.exe
290	-> Linux child, where `install -D /dev/stdin` sets mode and owner
291	atomically and creates the parent `.ssh/` directory. No plaintext
292	key on either side's disk, no remote temp file, no key material
293	in argv
294	.. {busc_call}
295	*Session 4 (shutdown)*: ssh as
296	{bujb_workload_user}
297	running `wsl.exe --shutdown` -- releases the WSL VM and helper-
298	process hive handles (wslhost.exe, wslservice) before this session's
299	Windows logon unloads. Best-effort defense against demotion
300	accumulation across the four sessions; Layer 1
301	({busn_caparison}-installed absolute-path Match block) is the auth
302	defense if a stray demotion occurs
303	.. {busc_call}
304	delete `«SEED_TARBALL»` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
305	.. {busc_fatal}
306	on any session's failure -- workload state partially provisioned
307	across namespaces; revalidate from a clean garrison
308	
309	//axhos_step
310	. *Lock down workload trust (admin rewrite)*:
311	.. {busc_call}
312	`Set-Content` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` overwriting the absolute-path
313	authorized_keys at
314	`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
315	with the locked-down line: directive prefix + `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»`
316	-- single cmdlet per WSG WSp-105; admin-owned file throughout; no
317	in-session rewrite race
318	.. Canonical directive prefix: `BUJB_command_w` in
319	{bujb_jurisdiction}
320	with `${BUJB_workload_user}` substituted at directive-emit time:
321	+
322	[listing]
323	----
324	command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding
325	----
326	.. {busc_fatal}
327	on rewrite failure -- bare trust still in place; subsequent
328	workload SSH would bypass the locked-down route; revalidate from a
329	clean garrison
330	
331	//axhog_guarantee
332	On success:
333	
334	* Exactly one workload identity named
335	{bujb_workload_user}
336	exists, mirrored across all three namespaces.
337	* Windows local user with SID registered under
338	`HKLM\\...\\ProfileList` (auth boundary).
339	* Workload-owned `rbtww-main` registered in workload's `HKCU\\Lxss`
340	(distribution namespace).
341	* WSL Linux user inside that distribution (execution context entered
342	by the locked-down `command=` directive's `wsl.exe --user`).
343	* Workload authorized_keys at
344	`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
345	carries `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` with the locked-down `command=`
346	directive -- admin-owned absolute path; auth decoupled from
347	workload `$HOME` via caparison-installed `Match User` block.
348	* {burp_workload_key_file}
349	planted at `/home/${BUJB_workload_user}/.ssh/id_ed25519` inside the
350	workload's distribution.
351	* `wsl.exe --shutdown` executed inside the workload session before
352	exit; no orphan `NTUSER.DAT` hive handles outlive garrison-w.
353	* Admin posture unchanged -- no `sshd_config` writes, no admin
354	authorized_keys writes.
355	
356	Garrison-w does not self-validate the locked-down round-trip;
357	{bust_invigilate_windows}
358	asserts it as a post-completion fact.
359	
360	//axhoc_completion
361	Exit 0 when step 12's admin-context lockdown rewrite completes
362	after step 11's `wsl.exe --shutdown` and seed cleanup. Non-zero on
363	any step's
364	{busc_fatal}.
365	
366	==== Rationale
367	
368	*Four-session shape with final shutdown (step 11)*::
369	WSG WSp-105 (one cmdlet or native binary per body) forbids the
370	compound session shape (wsl --import + useradd + install-D + wsl
371	--shutdown all on one workload SSH connection). Each cmdlet
372	therefore opens its own session. Across sessions 1-3, helper
373	processes (wslhost.exe, wslservice) spawned by `wsl --import`
374	outlive their parent session and lock the NTUSER.DAT hive of the
375	workload's logon, exposing the demotion path (User Profile Service
376	relocating the canonical profile to `bujuw_user.<host>.NNN` on the
377	next logon). Session 4 is `wsl.exe --shutdown` as its only act,
378	releasing the WSL VM and the helper-process hive handles before
379	that session's logon unloads -- best-effort cleanup that limits but
380	does not eliminate the demotion risk across sessions 1-3. Layer 1
381	({busn_caparison}-installed absolute-path Match block + admin-owned
382	authorized_keys) is the auth defense if demotion still occurs:
383	sshd reads as SYSTEM from the absolute path and never resolves the
384	workload's `$HOME`. The lockdown rewrite (step 12) runs in admin
385	context after session 4 exits -- no in-session rewrite race.
386	
387	*Two LIKE patterns in Phase 2*::
388	The query matches `LocalPath LIKE
389	'C:\Users\${BUJB_workload_user}%'` AND `LocalPath LIKE
390	'C:\Users\${BUJB_workload_user}.%'`. The first catches the
391	canonical path; the second catches numbered fallback dirs from
392	prior demotions (`bujuw_user.<host>.NNN`). One pattern alone leaks
393	state.
394	
395	*Per-row Remove-CimInstance in Phase 2*::
396	WSG WSp-105 mandates one cmdlet per SSH body. The probe emits
397	rows; bash iterates and issues one Remove-CimInstance per row with
398	a LocalPath-specific `-Query`. A piped pattern (probe |
399	remove-CimInstance) would violate WSp-105 and is forbidden.
400	
401	*Phase 5 is admin's rbtww-main, not workload's*::
402	Workload's distribution lives in workload's own `HKCU\Lxss`,
403	populated in step 11. Phase 5 cleans the workload's Linux user
404	vestige inside *admin's* `rbtww-main` -- vestige from prior
405	garrison shapes when workload shared admin's distribution. The
406	phase is retained because residual entries leak shell history and
407	HKCU pollution across garrisons.
408	
409	==== References
410	
411	* link:https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/openssh/openssh_server_configuration[Microsoft Learn -- OpenSSH Server Configuration for Windows] (AuthorizedKeysFile, Match blocks)
412	* link:https://github.com/PowerShell/Win32-OpenSSH/issues/1383[Win32-OpenSSH #1383] -- local-user SID registration under ProfileList required for OpenSSH logon
413	* link:https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-client/user-profiles-and-logon/profile-loading-fails[Microsoft Learn -- Profile loading fails]
414	* link:https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-client/setup-upgrade-and-drivers/scripts-clean-profile-folder-prevent-temp-profile[Microsoft Learn -- Scripts to clean profile folder and prevent TEMP user profile creation]
415	* link:https://helgeklein.com/free-tools/delprof2-user-profile-deletion-tool/[helgeklein.com -- Delprof2] -- documented authority for safe profile teardown sequencing
416	* WSG WSp-105 -- one cmdlet or one native binary per SSH body (no compound PowerShell bodies)
417
