1401	
1402	######################################################################
1403	# Internal: Caparison-windows helpers (BUSJCW — Windows OpenSSH only)
1404	#
1405	# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
1406	# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
1407	# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
1408	# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
1409	# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
1410	# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
1411	# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.
1412	
1413	# zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
1414	# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
1415	# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
1416	# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
1417	# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
1418	# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
1419	zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback() {
1420	  zbujb_sentinel
1421	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
1422	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
1423	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
1424	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"
1425	
1426	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"           \
1427	      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                \
1428	      -o BatchMode=no                            \
1429	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password \
1430	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"     \
1431	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"     \
1432	      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin}"             \
1433	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                       \
1434	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
1435	}
1436	
1437	# zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
1438	# Same as above but BatchMode=yes (no password fallback). Used for phase 2.
1439	zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly() {
1440	  zbujb_sentinel
1441	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
1442	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
1443	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly: stdout_file required"
1444	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly: stderr_file required"
1445	
1446	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"           \
1447	      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                \
1448	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"         \
1449	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey      \
1450	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"     \
1451	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"     \
1452	      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin}"             \
1453	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                       \
1454	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
1455	}
1456	
1457	# zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
1458	# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
1459	# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
1460	# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
1461	# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
1462	zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives() {
1463	  zbujb_sentinel
1464	  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
1465	  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file required"
1466	  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"
1467	
1468	  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
1469	  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
1470	  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"
1471	
1472	  local z_directives_roll=()
1473	  local z_pair=""
1474	  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
1475	    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
1476	    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
1477	  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
1478	
1479	  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
1480	  local z_line=""
1481	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
1482	    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
1483	  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"
1484	
1485	  local z_directive=""
1486	  local z_expected=""
1487	  local z_actual=""
1488	  local z_after_directive=""
1489	  local z_i=0
1490	  local z_j=0
1491	  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
1492	    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
1493	    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
1494	    z_actual=""
1495	
1496	    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
1497	      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
1498	        "${z_directive} "*)
1499	          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
1500	          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
1501	          break
1502	          ;;
1503	      esac
1504	    done
1505	
1506	    test -n "${z_actual}" \
1507	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
1508	    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
1509	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
1510	  done
1511	}
1512	
1513	# zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block REMOTE_FILE -- load raw sshd_config
1514	# bytes from REMOTE_FILE and assert the canonical Match User block is present:
1515	# the header line exactly matches "Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}", and the
1516	# next non-blank line is the AuthorizedKeysFile directive resolving to
1517	# ${BUJB_path_sshd_workload_authkeys}. Indentation on the AuthorizedKeysFile
1518	# line is stripped before comparison (sshd accepts indented continuations).
1519	# Failure pointer is BUSJCW (Match block is caparison-windows's deliverable);
1520	# invigilate reuses this helper for its sshd-config-shape fact.
1521	zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block() {
1522	  zbujb_sentinel
1523	  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
1524	  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block: remote_file required"
1525	  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"
1526	
1527	  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
1528	  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "Match block verify: empty remote sshd_config bytes (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
1529	  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"
1530	
1531	  local z_lines_roll=()
1532	  local z_line=""
1533	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
1534	    z_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
1535	  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"
1536	
1537	  local -r z_expected_header="Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}"
1538	  local -r z_expected_akf="AuthorizedKeysFile ${BUJB_path_sshd_workload_authkeys}"
1539	
1540	  local z_header_idx=-1
1541	  local z_i=0
1542	  for z_i in "${!z_lines_roll[@]}"; do
1543	    test "${z_lines_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_expected_header}" || continue
1544	    z_header_idx="${z_i}"
1545	    break
1546	  done
1547	
1548	  test "${z_header_idx}" -ge 0 \
1549	    || buc_die "Match block verify: header '${z_expected_header}' missing from sshd_config (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
1550	
1551	  # Find the next non-blank line after the header; strip leading whitespace
1552	  # before comparing to z_expected_akf.
1553	  local z_next_idx=$((z_header_idx + 1))
1554	  local z_max="${#z_lines_roll[@]}"
1555	  local z_candidate=""
1556	  local z_stripped=""
1557	  while test "${z_next_idx}" -lt "${z_max}"; do
1558	    z_candidate="${z_lines_roll[$z_next_idx]}"
1559	    z_stripped="${z_candidate#"${z_candidate%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
1560	    test -z "${z_stripped}" || break
1561	    z_next_idx=$((z_next_idx + 1))
1562	  done
1563	
1564	  test "${z_next_idx}" -lt "${z_max}" \
1565	    || buc_die "Match block verify: header '${z_expected_header}' present but no AuthorizedKeysFile directive follows (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
1566	  test "${z_stripped}" = "${z_expected_akf}" \
1567	    || buc_die "Match block verify: AuthorizedKeysFile: expected '${z_expected_akf}', got '${z_stripped}' (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
1568	}
1569	
1570	######################################################################
1571	# Internal: Caparison-windows phases
1572	
1573	# zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
1574	# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
1575	# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
1576	# expected — exit code ignored).
1577	zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1() {
1578	  zbujb_sentinel
1579	
1580	  ${BUJB_sshkeygen_emit_pubkey} "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" \
1581	      > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}" \
1582	      2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}" \
1583	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}"
1584	  local z_pubkey
1585	  z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}")
1586	  z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"
1587	
1588	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk A: pubkey + icacls + sshd_config + sshd -t + emit (operator may be prompted for admin password on first run)"
1589	
1590	  # Build the PS hashtable block from BUJB_sshd_hardening so the bash-side
1591	  # tinder is the sole source of truth for directive names + expected
1592	  # values; phase 2's verify and the PS-side rewrite cannot drift.
1593	  local z_ps_directives_block=""
1594	  local z_pair="" z_dir="" z_val=""
1595	  while IFS= read -r z_pair; do
1596	    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
1597	    z_dir="${z_pair%% *}"
1598	    z_val="${z_pair#* }"
1599	    z_ps_directives_block+="  '${z_dir}' = '${z_val}'"$'\n'
1600	  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
1601	
1602	  local z_idx_chunk_a
1603	  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_chunk_a
1604	  local -r z_chunk_a_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_a}_phase1_chunkA_stdout.txt"
1605	  local -r z_chunk_a_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_a}_phase1_chunkA_stderr.txt"
1606	  zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback \
1607	      "${z_chunk_a_stdout}"  \
1608	      "${z_chunk_a_stderr}"  \
1609	    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${z_chunk_a_stderr}"
1610	\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
1611	
1612	\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
1613	\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
1614	\$sshConfig    = "${BUJB_ps_sshd_config_path}"
1615	
1616	# Idempotent admin pubkey install
1617	if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
1618	  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
1619	}
1620	\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
1621	if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
1622	  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
1623	}
1624	
1625	# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
1626	icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_system}:F' /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_admins}:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
1627	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
1628	
1629	# Idempotent sshd_config harden — replace the first matching line for
1630	# each directive (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
1631	# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
1632	\$directives = [ordered]@{
1633	${z_ps_directives_block}}
1634	
1635	\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
1636	\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
1637	\$seen  = @{}
1638	foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
1639	  \$matched = \$false
1640	  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
1641	    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
1642	      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
1643	        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
1644	        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
1645	      }
1646	      \$matched = \$true
1647	      break
1648	    }
1649	  }
1650	  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
1651	}
1652	foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
1653	  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
1654	}
1655	Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII
1656	
1657	# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op; aborts before restart on bad config
1658	& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
1659	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
1660	
1661	# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (last op so output is clean)
1662	Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
1663	PS1
1664	
1665	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
1666	  zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives "${z_chunk_a_stdout}"
1667	
1668	  ##############
1669	  # Phase 1 extension — install Match User block + provision authkeys directory
1670	  # per BUSJCW step 5. Atomic single-cmdlet/single-binary SSH calls; bash owns
1671	  # the state machine via Capture-Decide-Dispatch.
1672	
1673	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Capture-Decide-Dispatch: Match User ${BUJB_workload_user} block"
1674	  local z_existing_sshd=""
1675	  z_existing_sshd=$(<"${z_chunk_a_stdout}")
1676	  # Normalize to LF endings — WriteAllBytes will write whatever we encode.
1677	  z_existing_sshd="${z_existing_sshd//$'\r'/}"
1678	
1679	  # Always strip any prior "Match User <workload>" block (lines from that
1680	  # header until the next "Match " line or EOF), then append the canonical
1681	  # block. This is nuclear-idempotent regardless of prior content.
1682	  local z_new_sshd=""
1683	  local z_skip_block=0
1684	  local z_line=""
1685	  local z_existing_lines_roll=()
1686	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
1687	    z_existing_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
1688	  done <<<"${z_existing_sshd}"
1689	
1690	  local z_i=0
1691	  for z_i in "${!z_existing_lines_roll[@]}"; do
1692	    z_line="${z_existing_lines_roll[$z_i]}"
1693	    case "${z_line}" in
1694	      "Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}")
1695	        z_skip_block=1
1696	        continue
1697	        ;;
1698	      "Match "*)
1699	        z_skip_block=0
1700	        ;;
1701	    esac
1702	    test "${z_skip_block}" -eq 1 || z_new_sshd+="${z_line}"$'\n'
1703	  done
1704	
1705	  # Append canonical Match block (trailing newline so any future appends
1706	  # start on a fresh line).
1707	  z_new_sshd+="${BUJB_sshd_match_block_text}"$'\n'
1708	
1709	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Encode new sshd_config bytes for transport"
1710	  printf '%s' "${z_new_sshd}"                                  \
1711	      | openssl enc -base64 -A                                 \
1712	          > "${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDOUT}"                     \
1713	          2> "${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDERR}"                    \
1714	    || buc_die "Phase 1: base64 encode of sshd_config failed — see ${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDERR}"
1715	  local z_sshd_b64=""
1716	  z_sshd_b64=$(<"${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDOUT}")
1717	  z_sshd_b64="${z_sshd_b64//$'\n'/}"
1718	
1719	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Write new sshd_config with Match block"
1720	  zbujb_caparison_run "p1-sshd-config-write" \
1721	      zbujb_admin_powershell "[System.IO.File]::WriteAllBytes((\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\sshd_config'), [System.Convert]::FromBase64String('${z_sshd_b64}'))" \
1722	    || buc_die "Phase 1: failed to write sshd_config with Match block"
1723	
1724	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] CDD: workload authkeys directory presence"
1725	  local z_dir_present=""
1726	  z_dir_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
1727	      "Test-Path (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}')") \
1728	    || buc_die "Phase 1: Test-Path on workload authkeys directory failed"
1729	  z_dir_present="${z_dir_present//[$'\r\n']/}"
1730	  case "${z_dir_present}" in
1731	    True)
1732	      ;;
1733	    False)
1734	      buc_step "  [Phase 1] Create workload authkeys directory"
1735	      zbujb_caparison_run "p1-mkdir-authkeys-dir" \
1736	          zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}') -Force | Out-Null" \
1737	        || buc_die "Phase 1: failed to create workload authkeys directory"
1738	      ;;
1739	    *)
1740	      buc_die "Phase 1: Test-Path returned unexpected '${z_dir_present}' for workload authkeys directory"
1741	      ;;
1742	  esac
1743	
1744	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] icacls workload authkeys directory (admins+SYSTEM FullControl, inheritance disabled)"
1745	  zbujb_caparison_run "p1-icacls-authkeys-dir" \
1746	      zbujb_admin_powershell "icacls (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}') /inheritance:r /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_system}:F' /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_admins}:F'" \
1747	    || buc_die "Phase 1: icacls on workload authkeys directory failed"
1748	
1749	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] sshd -t (re-validate after Match block append)"
1750	  zbujb_caparison_run "p1-sshd-t-rev" \
1751	      zbujb_admin_powershell "& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t" \
1752	    || buc_die "Phase 1: sshd -t failed after Match block append — config malformed"
1753	
1754	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Re-read sshd_config and verify Match block resolved"
1755	  local z_idx_verify
1756	  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_verify
1757	  local -r z_verify_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_verify}_phase1_match_verify_stdout.txt"
1758	  local -r z_verify_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_verify}_phase1_match_verify_stderr.txt"
1759	  zbujb_admin_powershell "Get-Content (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\sshd_config') -Raw" \
1760	      > "${z_verify_stdout}" \
1761	      2> "${z_verify_stderr}" \
1762	    || buc_die "Phase 1: Get-Content sshd_config (verify pass) failed — see ${z_verify_stderr}"
1763	  zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block "${z_verify_stdout}"
1764	
1765	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk B: Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — exit code ignored)"
1766	  local z_idx_chunk_b
1767	  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_chunk_b
1768	  local -r z_chunk_b_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_b}_phase1_chunkB_stdout.txt"
1769	  local -r z_chunk_b_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_b}_phase1_chunkB_stderr.txt"
1770	  zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback \
1771	      "${z_chunk_b_stdout}"  \
1772	      "${z_chunk_b_stderr}"  \
1773	    <<'PS1' || true
1774	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
1775	Restart-Service sshd
1776	PS1
1777	}
1778	
1779	# zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
1780	# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
1781	zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2() {
1782	  zbujb_sentinel
1783	
1784	  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"
1785	
1786	  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
1787	  sleep 3
1788	
1789	  local z_idx_phase2
1790	  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_phase2
1791	  local -r z_phase2_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_phase2}_phase2_stdout.txt"
1792	  local -r z_phase2_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_phase2}_phase2_stderr.txt"
1793	  zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly  \
1794	      "${z_phase2_stdout}"  \
1795	      "${z_phase2_stderr}"  \
1796	    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${z_phase2_stderr}"
1797	\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
1798	Get-Content "${BUJB_ps_sshd_config_path}" -Raw
1799	PS1

file.content:
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Caparison-windows helpers (BUSJCW — Windows OpenSSH only)
#
# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.

# zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"           \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                \
      -o BatchMode=no                            \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"     \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"     \
      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin}"             \
      > "${z_stdout_file}"                       \
      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
}

# zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
# Same as above but BatchMode=yes (no password fallback). Used for phase 2.
zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly: stdout_file required"
  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly: stderr_file required"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"           \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"         \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey      \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"     \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"     \
      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_file_stdin}"             \
      > "${z_stdout_file}"                       \
      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
}

# zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file required"
  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"

  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"

  local z_directives_roll=()
  local z_pair=""
  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"

  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"

  local z_directive=""
  local z_expected=""
  local z_actual=""
  local z_after_directive=""
  local z_i=0
  local z_j=0
  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
    z_actual=""

    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
        "${z_directive} "*)
          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
          break
          ;;
      esac
    done

    test -n "${z_actual}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
  done
}

# zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block REMOTE_FILE -- load raw sshd_config
# bytes from REMOTE_FILE and assert the canonical Match User block is present:
# the header line exactly matches "Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}", and the
# next non-blank line is the AuthorizedKeysFile directive resolving to
# ${BUJB_path_sshd_workload_authkeys}. Indentation on the AuthorizedKeysFile
# line is stripped before comparison (sshd accepts indented continuations).
# Failure pointer is BUSJCW (Match block is caparison-windows's deliverable);
# invigilate reuses this helper for its sshd-config-shape fact.
zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block: remote_file required"
  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"

  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "Match block verify: empty remote sshd_config bytes (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"

  local z_lines_roll=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"

  local -r z_expected_header="Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}"
  local -r z_expected_akf="AuthorizedKeysFile ${BUJB_path_sshd_workload_authkeys}"

  local z_header_idx=-1
  local z_i=0
  for z_i in "${!z_lines_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_lines_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_expected_header}" || continue
    z_header_idx="${z_i}"
    break
  done

  test "${z_header_idx}" -ge 0 \
    || buc_die "Match block verify: header '${z_expected_header}' missing from sshd_config (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"

  # Find the next non-blank line after the header; strip leading whitespace
  # before comparing to z_expected_akf.
  local z_next_idx=$((z_header_idx + 1))
  local z_max="${#z_lines_roll[@]}"
  local z_candidate=""
  local z_stripped=""
  while test "${z_next_idx}" -lt "${z_max}"; do
    z_candidate="${z_lines_roll[$z_next_idx]}"
    z_stripped="${z_candidate#"${z_candidate%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    test -z "${z_stripped}" || break
    z_next_idx=$((z_next_idx + 1))
  done

  test "${z_next_idx}" -lt "${z_max}" \
    || buc_die "Match block verify: header '${z_expected_header}' present but no AuthorizedKeysFile directive follows (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
  test "${z_stripped}" = "${z_expected_akf}" \
    || buc_die "Match block verify: AuthorizedKeysFile: expected '${z_expected_akf}', got '${z_stripped}' (see ${z_remote_file}) — caparison-windows (BUSJCW)"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Caparison-windows phases

# zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
# expected — exit code ignored).
zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  ${BUJB_sshkeygen_emit_pubkey} "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" \
      > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}" \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}"
  local z_pubkey
  z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}")
  z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk A: pubkey + icacls + sshd_config + sshd -t + emit (operator may be prompted for admin password on first run)"

  # Build the PS hashtable block from BUJB_sshd_hardening so the bash-side
  # tinder is the sole source of truth for directive names + expected
  # values; phase 2's verify and the PS-side rewrite cannot drift.
  local z_ps_directives_block=""
  local z_pair="" z_dir="" z_val=""
  while IFS= read -r z_pair; do
    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
    z_dir="${z_pair%% *}"
    z_val="${z_pair#* }"
    z_ps_directives_block+="  '${z_dir}' = '${z_val}'"$'\n'
  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"

  local z_idx_chunk_a
  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_chunk_a
  local -r z_chunk_a_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_a}_phase1_chunkA_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_chunk_a_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_a}_phase1_chunkA_stderr.txt"
  zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback \
      "${z_chunk_a_stdout}"  \
      "${z_chunk_a_stderr}"  \
    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${z_chunk_a_stderr}"
\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
\$sshConfig    = "${BUJB_ps_sshd_config_path}"

# Idempotent admin pubkey install
if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
}
\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
}

# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_system}:F' /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_admins}:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Idempotent sshd_config harden — replace the first matching line for
# each directive (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
\$directives = [ordered]@{
${z_ps_directives_block}}

\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
\$seen  = @{}
foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
  \$matched = \$false
  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
      }
      \$matched = \$true
      break
    }
  }
  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
}
foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
}
Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII

# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op; aborts before restart on bad config
& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (last op so output is clean)
Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
PS1

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
  zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives "${z_chunk_a_stdout}"

  ##############
  # Phase 1 extension — install Match User block + provision authkeys directory
  # per BUSJCW step 5. Atomic single-cmdlet/single-binary SSH calls; bash owns
  # the state machine via Capture-Decide-Dispatch.

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Capture-Decide-Dispatch: Match User ${BUJB_workload_user} block"
  local z_existing_sshd=""
  z_existing_sshd=$(<"${z_chunk_a_stdout}")
  # Normalize to LF endings — WriteAllBytes will write whatever we encode.
  z_existing_sshd="${z_existing_sshd//$'\r'/}"

  # Always strip any prior "Match User <workload>" block (lines from that
  # header until the next "Match " line or EOF), then append the canonical
  # block. This is nuclear-idempotent regardless of prior content.
  local z_new_sshd=""
  local z_skip_block=0
  local z_line=""
  local z_existing_lines_roll=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_existing_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
  done <<<"${z_existing_sshd}"

  local z_i=0
  for z_i in "${!z_existing_lines_roll[@]}"; do
    z_line="${z_existing_lines_roll[$z_i]}"
    case "${z_line}" in
      "Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}")
        z_skip_block=1
        continue
        ;;
      "Match "*)
        z_skip_block=0
        ;;
    esac
    test "${z_skip_block}" -eq 1 || z_new_sshd+="${z_line}"$'\n'
  done

  # Append canonical Match block (trailing newline so any future appends
  # start on a fresh line).
  z_new_sshd+="${BUJB_sshd_match_block_text}"$'\n'

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Encode new sshd_config bytes for transport"
  printf '%s' "${z_new_sshd}"                                  \
      | openssl enc -base64 -A                                 \
          > "${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDOUT}"                     \
          2> "${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDERR}"                    \
    || buc_die "Phase 1: base64 encode of sshd_config failed — see ${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDERR}"
  local z_sshd_b64=""
  z_sshd_b64=$(<"${ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDOUT}")
  z_sshd_b64="${z_sshd_b64//$'\n'/}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Write new sshd_config with Match block"
  zbujb_caparison_run "p1-sshd-config-write" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell "[System.IO.File]::WriteAllBytes((\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\sshd_config'), [System.Convert]::FromBase64String('${z_sshd_b64}'))" \
    || buc_die "Phase 1: failed to write sshd_config with Match block"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] CDD: workload authkeys directory presence"
  local z_dir_present=""
  z_dir_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged \
      "Test-Path (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}')") \
    || buc_die "Phase 1: Test-Path on workload authkeys directory failed"
  z_dir_present="${z_dir_present//[$'\r\n']/}"
  case "${z_dir_present}" in
    True)
      ;;
    False)
      buc_step "  [Phase 1] Create workload authkeys directory"
      zbujb_caparison_run "p1-mkdir-authkeys-dir" \
          zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}') -Force | Out-Null" \
        || buc_die "Phase 1: failed to create workload authkeys directory"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Phase 1: Test-Path returned unexpected '${z_dir_present}' for workload authkeys directory"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] icacls workload authkeys directory (admins+SYSTEM FullControl, inheritance disabled)"
  zbujb_caparison_run "p1-icacls-authkeys-dir" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell "icacls (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}') /inheritance:r /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_system}:F' /grant '${BUJB_acl_principal_admins}:F'" \
    || buc_die "Phase 1: icacls on workload authkeys directory failed"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] sshd -t (re-validate after Match block append)"
  zbujb_caparison_run "p1-sshd-t-rev" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell "& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t" \
    || buc_die "Phase 1: sshd -t failed after Match block append — config malformed"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Re-read sshd_config and verify Match block resolved"
  local z_idx_verify
  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_verify
  local -r z_verify_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_verify}_phase1_match_verify_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_verify_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_verify}_phase1_match_verify_stderr.txt"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Get-Content (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\sshd_config') -Raw" \
      > "${z_verify_stdout}" \
      2> "${z_verify_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Phase 1: Get-Content sshd_config (verify pass) failed — see ${z_verify_stderr}"
  zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_match_block "${z_verify_stdout}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk B: Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — exit code ignored)"
  local z_idx_chunk_b
  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_chunk_b
  local -r z_chunk_b_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_b}_phase1_chunkB_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_chunk_b_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_chunk_b}_phase1_chunkB_stderr.txt"
  zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_with_password_fallback \
      "${z_chunk_b_stdout}"  \
      "${z_chunk_b_stderr}"  \
    <<'PS1' || true
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
Restart-Service sshd
PS1
}

# zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"

  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
  sleep 3

  local z_idx_phase2
  zbujb_emit_index_advance z_idx_phase2
  local -r z_phase2_stdout="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_phase2}_phase2_stdout.txt"
  local -r z_phase2_stderr="${ZBUJB_CAPARISON_PREFIX}${z_idx_phase2}_phase2_stderr.txt"
  zbujb_caparison_windows_exec_keyonly  \
      "${z_phase2_stdout}"  \
      "${z_phase2_stderr}"  \
    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${z_phase2_stderr}"
\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
Get-Content "${BUJB_ps_sshd_config_path}" -Raw
PS1

zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives "${z_phase2_stdout}"
}

# zbujb_caparison_windows_phase3 -- post-trust admin posture: stage the
# canonical WSL distribution, disable powercfg sleep/hibernate, and set
# the Tailscale service to Automatic + running. WSL stage delegates to
# zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl. Each remaining op is one
# zbujb_admin_powershell call routed through zbujb_caparison_run
# so per-call evidence lands at the autonumbered file. On failure the
# wrapper's diag preview already names the file; buc_die just adds the
# BUSJHW pointer.
zbujb_caparison_windows_phase3() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  [Phase 3] Stage ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} WSL distribution"
  zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl

  buc_step "  [Phase 3] Disable standby (AC)"
  zbujb_caparison_run "powercfg-standby-ac" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell 'powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0' \
    || buc_die "Phase 3: powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0 failed — BUSJHW (Modern Standby AoAc override may demote to no-op)"

  buc_step "  [Phase 3] Disable standby (DC)"
  zbujb_caparison_run "powercfg-standby-dc" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell 'powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0' \
    || buc_die "Phase 3: powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0 failed — BUSJHW (Modern Standby AoAc override may demote to no-op)"

  buc_step "  [Phase 3] Disable hibernate"
  zbujb_caparison_run "powercfg-hibernate-off" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell 'powercfg /hibernate off' \
    || buc_die "Phase 3: powercfg /hibernate off failed — BUSJHW (Modern Standby AoAc override may demote to no-op)"

  buc_step "  [Phase 3] Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic"
  zbujb_caparison_run "tailscale-set-service" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell 'Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic' \
    || buc_die "Phase 3: Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic failed — BUSJHW (Tailscale install + Run-Unattended + first auth)"

  buc_step "  [Phase 3] Start Tailscale service"
  zbujb_caparison_run "tailscale-start-service" \
      zbujb_admin_powershell 'Start-Service -Name Tailscale' \
    || buc_die "Phase 3: Start-Service -Name Tailscale failed — BUSJHW (Tailscale install + Run-Unattended + first auth)"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Caparison-windows ceremony

# bujb_caparison_windows -- run the three-phase caparison-windows ceremony
# for a Windows OpenSSH node. Phase 1: admin pubkey install + sshd_config
# harden + sshd -t + Restart-Service sshd. Phase 2: reconnect under
# key-only auth + re-verify directives. Phase 3: WSL distribution stage +
# powercfg sleep/hibernate disable + Tailscale service auto-start. Closes
# with post-completion invigilate-windows. Caller must have invoked
# bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_caparison_windows() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_caparison_windows: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "bujb_caparison_windows: requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"

  buc_step "Caparison-windows: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1
  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2

  buc_step "  [Preflight] Operator-handbook preconditions (gate before phase 3 WSL stage)"
  zbujb_invigilate_windows_op_facts

  zbujb_caparison_windows_phase3

  buc_step "  Post-completion: verify caparison deliverables"
  zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts

  buc_step "Caparison-windows succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: WSL stage helper (invoked by bujb_caparison_windows phase 3)

# zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl -- idempotently provision
# BUJB_wsl_distribution by purging any prior state, installing an
# Ubuntu-24.04 seed, exporting it to a .tar, importing under the
# canonical name, then unregistering the seed and removing the .tar.
# Caller is bujb_caparison_windows phase 3, which has already asserted
# resolve + bubep_windows. Each step propagates failure via
# zbujb_admin_powershell + || buc_die.
zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  local -r z_distro_dir="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  local -r z_tar_path="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}.tar"

  buc_step "  Stage WSL: ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} on ${BURN_HOST} (seed: ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution})"

  buc_step "  [1/6] Purge prior state (idempotent: unregister both, remove tar+dir)"
  local z_wsl_list=""
  z_wsl_list=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "wsl.exe --list --quiet") \
    || z_wsl_list=""
  if grep -qFx "${BUJB_wsl_distribution}" <<<"${z_wsl_list}"; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to unregister prior ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  fi
  if grep -qFx "${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" <<<"${z_wsl_list}"; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to unregister prior ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  fi
  local z_tar_present z_dir_present
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  z_dir_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_distro_dir}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_distro_dir}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove prior ${z_tar_path}"
  fi
  if [[ "${z_dir_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '${z_distro_dir}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove prior ${z_distro_dir}"
  fi

  buc_step "  [2/6] Ensure ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root} directory"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path '${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}' -Force | Out-Null" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}"

  buc_step "  [3/6] Install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed distribution"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed"

  buc_step "  [4/6] Export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} to ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [5/6] Import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} from ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} '${z_distro_dir}' '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [6/6] Cleanup: unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} and remove .tar"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove ${z_tar_path}"
  fi

  buc_step "  Stage WSL succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Privileged SSH (thin admin-side pass-through)

# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
# ssh's exit code.
bujb_privileged_ssh() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: command required"

  buc_step "Privileged SSH: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"           \

**Heat context**: ₣A- (caparison-windows-decompose's parent heat is Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle; jurisdiction-spine work). Field state on bujn-winpc has sshd_config reverted to clean post-merge content; rbtww-main unregistered; C:\WSL swept; C:\bujb-wsl awaits Phase 3 provisioning.

**Silks assessment**: `caparison-windows-decompose` fits — mechanical decomposition guided by locked decisions, no rename needed.

**Current state**:
- BUSJCW spec is already in axhos_step orchestration form (15 steps total; Phase 1 = 8 steps); needs Phase 1 restructure to ~13 single-cmdlet steps with the key-auth probe pattern.
- `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` phase 1 (~lines 1577-1777) has chunk A as a multi-statement heredoc PS script + bash-side CDD additions, chunk B as a separate heredoc; targets for deletion are at lines 212, 1413-1455.
- Phase 2 currently uses `_exec_keyonly` with a heredoc; the simple `zbujb_admin_powershell` already does key-auth.

**Proposed approach (4 bullets)**:
- **Spec first**: Rewrite BUSJCW Phase 1 (current 8 axhos_steps) into ~13 single-cmdlet steps matching BUSJGW's orchestration form: key-auth probe → conditional password-fallback Add-Content → decomposed admin-pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/Match block/sshd -t/restart, each as one `zbujb_admin_powershell` call. Phase 2/3 spec mostly intact.
- **Bash second**: Rewrite `zbujb_caparison_windows_phase1` to match: key-auth probe via `zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0'`, conditional inline ssh-with-password-fallback for the single `Add-Content`, then sequential `zbujb_admin_powershell` calls. Rewrite `zbujb_caparison_windows_phase2` to use `zbujb_admin_powershell`. Delete the three named symbols.
- **Gates**: `bash -n bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`, `tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.handbook-render.sh`, `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.

content.text:
Sorry, that was too compressed. Here's the fuller picture for Phase 2:

**Current Phase 2 (lines 1781-1802) does two things:**
1. **Reconnect under key-only auth** — the implicit "prove it works by not failing" assertion. This is the structural raison d'être of Phase 2 per the standing note about Restart-Service killing the session.
2. **Re-read sshd_config and verify hardened directives** — substantive post-restart deliverable: confirms the file on disk still parses to the expected directives after the restart cycle.

**What that verify catches**: directive drift between pre-restart write and post-restart on-disk state (e.g., another process rewrote sshd_config in the gap; PS rewrite landed differently than expected). It does NOT verify the running daemon's in-memory config — only Get-Content of the file. The successful key-auth reconnect IS the proof of the running daemon's auth posture.

**Two real shapes:**

- **Option A — Single Get-Content + bash verify**: Phase 2 stays as one `zbujb_admin_powershell "Get-Content (\$env:ProgramData + '\\ssh\\sshd_config') -Raw"` (instead of the current heredoc-fed `_exec_keyonly`), captured to a temp file, then bash-side `verify_directives` + `verify_match_block` (re-read after Match block landed). One axhos_step in spec; preserves the fast-fail forensic if directive drift surfaces in the gap.

- **Option B — Probe-only handshake**: Phase 2 = just `zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0'`. Deliverables verify moves entirely to `zbujb_invigilate_windows_caparison_facts` (already invoked post-phase 3). Slimmer Phase 2; but Phase 3's multi-minute WSL stage and powercfg/Tailscale ops would run before the directive-drift fast-fail surfaces.

**Recommendation: Option A.** The verify costs one ssh call but tightens the failure window — directive drift surfaces immediately after restart, not after the WSL stage burns minutes. The WSp-105 single-cmdlet shape (Get-Content as the sole body) is clean. The existing `verify_directives` + `verify_match_block` helpers stay intact.

Ready to proceed with that? Then I'll set up task tracking and start with the spec rewrite.

input.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_caparison_windows}

NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
key trust + `sshd_config` hardening, decomposed into single-cmdlet
calls over the admin-shell helper in {bujb_jurisdiction} bounded by a
key-auth CDD probe so steady-state re-runs are key-only end-to-end
and only first-caparison-ever on a host issues a single TTY password
prompt for the admin pubkey install. Phase 2: key-only reconnect +
served-config re-verify. Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL
distribution, powercfg, Tailscale service). Closes with
{bust_invigilate_windows}.

NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
the idempotent admin-shell remainder.

NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
failure. Every Phase 1 PowerShell body is one cmdlet or one native-
binary call per WSG WSp-105; the wrapper-side prelude/postlude lives
in {bujb_jurisdiction}'s admin-shell helpers, not in caller bodies.
Bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config`
directives from the raw bytes emitted by `Get-Content -Raw`.

NOTE: Password-fallback transport is bounded to a single `Add-Content`
cmdlet (admin pubkey install) gated by the key-auth CDD probe. On
first-caparison-ever the probe returns False and the bounded session
prompts on /dev/tty; every re-run sees the probe return True and the
install step no-ops because the admin pubkey is by construction
already authorized. Every other Phase 1 step is key-only via
{bujb_jurisdiction}'s admin-shell helper.

The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
Caparison presupposes this scope.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- derive admin pubkey*:
.. {busc_call}
`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file} (curia-side)
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
.. {busc_fatal}
on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- probe key-auth availability (CDD probe)*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using
{burp_privileged_key_file}, running `exit 0` via the admin-shell
helper -- single-statement PS body per WSG WSp-105
.. {busc_store}
`«KEY_AUTH_AVAILABLE»` = `True` on exit 0, `False` on transport or
auth failure (absorbed silently -- both outcomes are valid
pre-conditions; first-caparison-ever vs. re-run)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- install admin pubkey if absent (CDD dispatch)*:
.. On `«KEY_AUTH_AVAILABLE»` equal to `False`, {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (`BatchMode=no` allows
the /dev/tty password prompt on first-caparison-ever; operator types
once; caparison never sees the password), running a single
`Add-Content -Path "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
-Value '«ADMIN_PUBKEY»' -Encoding ASCII` over a password-fallback
admin-shell helper -- single cmdlet body per WSG WSp-105.
`Add-Content` creates the file when absent
.. On `«KEY_AUTH_AVAILABLE»` equal to `True`, no-op -- the admin
pubkey is by construction already authorized (proven by step 2's
success)
.. {busc_fatal}
on `Add-Content` failure -- write denied, file locked, or
operator-typed password rejected

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
.. {busc_call}
`icacls "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
/inheritance:r /grant 'NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM:F' /grant
'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F'` over key-only ssh -- Windows OpenSSH
refuses the file otherwise; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `icacls` failure -- subsequent key-auth ops would not authenticate

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- read current sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config" -Raw` over key-only
ssh -- raw bytes for curia-side construction; single cmdlet
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_CURRENT»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on read failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (sshd_config missing
or unreadable)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- build new sshd_config content (curia-side)*:
.. strip CR bytes from `«SSHD_CONFIG_CURRENT»` and walk lines: for
each hardening directive (`PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`), replace the
first matching line (commented or otherwise) with the canonical
value and drop subsequent duplicates; append any absent directive
.. strip any prior `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block (lines
from that header to the next `Match ` line or EOF), then append the
canonical block:
+
[listing]
----
Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}
    AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys
----
.. the absolute path decouples sshd's workload authorized_keys
lookup from workload's `$HOME`, so a future profile demotion cannot
lock out auth; parallel to the built-in `Match Group administrators`
block routing to `administrators_authorized_keys`
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_NEW»` (curia bytes)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- write new sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
base64-encode `«SSHD_CONFIG_NEW»` on curia, then over key-only ssh
run `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllBytes("$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config",
[System.Convert]::FromBase64String('<b64>'))` -- single-statement PS
body. Base64 transport avoids in-flight encoding drift across the
cmd.exe argv layer
.. {busc_fatal}
on WriteAllBytes failure -- restart would still serve unhardened auth

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- provision workload authkeys parent directory (CDD)*:
.. {busc_call}
`Test-Path "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"`
over key-only ssh
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_AUTHKEYS_DIR_PRESENT»` = `True` or `False`
.. On `«WORKLOAD_AUTHKEYS_DIR_PRESENT»` equal to `False`, {busc_call}
`New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path
"$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}" -Force |
Out-Null` over key-only ssh -- single cmdlet. The directory persists
across {busn_garrison} destroy-and-recreate cycles -- garrison
rewrites the file contents but never touches the directory
.. {busc_fatal}
on probe or create failure -- workload auth path would not resolve

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- lock down workload authkeys parent directory ACL*:
.. {busc_call}
`icacls "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
/inheritance:r /grant 'NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM:F' /grant
'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F'` over key-only ssh -- admins/SYSTEM Full
Control, inheritance disabled, no workload grant; sshd reads as
SYSTEM, parallel to administrators_authorized_keys discipline;
idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `icacls` failure -- sshd would refuse the workload trust file

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- validate sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`& "C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe" -t` over key-only ssh
-- single-statement PS body; penultimate atomic op of phase 1
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- re-read sshd_config (post-write)*:
.. {busc_call}
`Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config" -Raw` over key-only
ssh
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_VERIFY»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on read failure -- post-write state unverifiable

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- verify directives and Match block (curia-side)*:
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_VERIFY»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
directive values (BCG-compliant matching) AND contains a `Match User
${BUJB_workload_user}` block whose `AuthorizedKeysFile` resolves to
`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
.. {busc_fatal}
on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- restart sshd*:
.. {busc_call}
`Restart-Service sshd` over key-only ssh -- final atomic op of
phase 1; admin pubkey is installed by step 3 and authkeys ACL
locked down by step 4, so key auth is available for the
restart-issuing call itself
.. {busc_fatal}
on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
expected phase boundary, not a failure)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 -- reconnect + re-verify served sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config" -Raw` over key-only
ssh -- the successful reconnect IS the proof of key-auth restoration
after restart; the Get-Content body satisfies WSG WSp-105 with a
single cmdlet
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_SERVED»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_SERVED»` parses to the three hardening directives AND
the canonical `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block (BCG-compliant
matching; same shape as step 12)
.. {busc_fatal}
on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
session); recover via operator's local console. On directive or
Match-block mismatch -- post-restart drift between pre-restart write
and on-disk state; fast-fail forensic before phase 3's multi-minute
WSL stage burns

//axhos_step
. *Preflight -- verify operator-precondition facts before phase 3*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} operator-precondition fact subset
(`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` registry, `PlatformAoAcOverride`
registry, Tailscale service registered) over key-only ssh
.. {busc_store}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope; preflight fails fast
before the multi-minute WSL stage so missing operator posture does
not waste a download cycle

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- provision C:\\bujb-wsl directory*:
.. {busc_call}
`New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'C:\\bujb-wsl' -Force` over
key-only ssh -- idempotent. Holds admin's `rbtww-main` distribution
VHD tree AND serves as the admin↔workload handoff point for the
seed tarball that {busn_garrison} (WSL letter) writes during step
3. Default ACL inheritance from `C:\\` grants
`BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`, which is what allows the workload
SSH session in garrison-w step 11 to read the seed -- the seed must
NOT live under workload's Windows profile because admin's
`wsl --export` writes file ACLs that exclude the workload user
.. {busc_fatal}
on directory-creation failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
.. {busc_call}
`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over key-only ssh to
register `rbtww-main` under the admin user at
`C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main` (canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in
{bujb_jurisdiction})
.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
.. {busc_fatal}
on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
.. {busc_call}
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
`powercfg /hibernate off` over key-only ssh; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
.. {busc_call}
`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over key-only ssh; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)

//axhos_step
. *Post-completion -- verify caparison deliverables*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} caparison-deliverable fact subset
(Tailscale service `Automatic`, standby-timeout policy = 0 AC+DC,
hibernate disabled, `rbtww-main` WSL distribution registered) over
key-only ssh. Operator-precondition facts already verified at
preflight do not re-run, and admin SSH is not re-probed because
phase 2 just established it
.. {busc_store}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim

//axhog_guarantee
On success:

* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`, AND a
  `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block routing
  `AuthorizedKeysFile` to the admin-owned absolute path
  `__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`.
* `__PROGRAMDATA__\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}\\` directory
  exists with admins/SYSTEM FullControl, inheritance disabled, no
  workload grant -- sshd reads as SYSTEM, ready for {busn_garrison}
  to write the trust file content (the file itself is garrison's
  responsibility, not caparison's).
* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone; both Phase
  2's Get-Content reconnect and Phase 3's key-only ops confirm this.
* `C:\\bujb-wsl\\` directory exists (admin↔workload handoff point;
  default-inherited ACL grants workload `BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`).
* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user
  at `C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main`.
* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
* {bust_invigilate_windows}'s caparison-deliverable fact subset
  confirms each post-trust posture fact (operator-precondition facts
  were verified at the phase 2/3 preflight gate); a standalone
  {bust_invigilate_windows} run after caparison succeeds asserts the
  full fact list end-to-end.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the post-completion deliverables-subset
{bust_invigilate_windows} passes. Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure
(the key-auth probe is non-fatal -- both `True` and `False` are
valid pre-conditions -- but admin pubkey install, ACL, sshd_config
read/build/write, workload authkeys dir provision/ACL, `sshd -t`,
post-write verify, and restart can each fatal), phase-2 reconnect
or served-config-verify failure, preflight failure
(operator-precondition facts), any phase-3 op failure (`wsl
--import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
deliverables-subset failure.

toolUseResult.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_caparison_windows}

NOTE: {busn_caparison} on Windows -- three phases. Phase 1: admin SSH
key trust + `sshd_config` hardening, decomposed into single-cmdlet
calls over the admin-shell helper in {bujb_jurisdiction} bounded by a
key-auth CDD probe so steady-state re-runs are key-only end-to-end
and only first-caparison-ever on a host issues a single TTY password
prompt for the admin pubkey install. Phase 2: key-only reconnect +
served-config re-verify. Phase 3: post-trust admin posture (WSL
distribution, powercfg, Tailscale service). Closes with
{bust_invigilate_windows}.

NOTE: Nuclear -- every invocation purges and re-establishes the
caparison-managed posture; no skip-if-present logic.
{bust_handbook_windows} owns operator-manual posture; caparison owns
the idempotent admin-shell remainder.

NOTE: Phase 1's `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session
-- the post-restart disconnect is the expected phase boundary, not a
failure. Every Phase 1 PowerShell body is one cmdlet or one native-
binary call per WSG WSp-105; the wrapper-side prelude/postlude lives
in {bujb_jurisdiction}'s admin-shell helpers, not in caller bodies.
Bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts effective `sshd_config`
directives from the raw bytes emitted by `Get-Content -Raw`.

NOTE: Password-fallback transport is bounded to a single `Add-Content`
cmdlet (admin pubkey install) gated by the key-auth CDD probe. On
first-caparison-ever the probe returns False and the bounded session
prompts on /dev/tty; every re-run sees the probe return True and the
install step no-ops because the admin pubkey is by construction
already authorized. Every other Phase 1 step is key-only via
{bujb_jurisdiction}'s admin-shell helper.

The {busn_caparison} operation on Windows:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
{burn_regime} targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
Caparison presupposes this scope.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- derive admin pubkey*:
.. {busc_call}
`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file} (curia-side)
.. {busc_store}
`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
.. {busc_fatal}
on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- probe key-auth availability (CDD probe)*:
.. {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using
{burp_privileged_key_file}, running `exit 0` via the admin-shell
helper -- single-statement PS body per WSG WSp-105
.. {busc_store}
`«KEY_AUTH_AVAILABLE»` = `True` on exit 0, `False` on transport or
auth failure (absorbed silently -- both outcomes are valid
pre-conditions; first-caparison-ever vs. re-run)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- install admin pubkey if absent (CDD dispatch)*:
.. On `«KEY_AUTH_AVAILABLE»` equal to `False`, {busc_call}
ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (`BatchMode=no` allows
the /dev/tty password prompt on first-caparison-ever; operator types
once; caparison never sees the password), running a single
`Add-Content -Path "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
-Value '«ADMIN_PUBKEY»' -Encoding ASCII` over a password-fallback
admin-shell helper -- single cmdlet body per WSG WSp-105.
`Add-Content` creates the file when absent
.. On `«KEY_AUTH_AVAILABLE»` equal to `True`, no-op -- the admin
pubkey is by construction already authorized (proven by step 2's
success)
.. {busc_fatal}
on `Add-Content` failure -- write denied, file locked, or
operator-typed password rejected

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
.. {busc_call}
`icacls "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
/inheritance:r /grant 'NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM:F' /grant
'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F'` over key-only ssh -- Windows OpenSSH
refuses the file otherwise; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `icacls` failure -- subsequent key-auth ops would not authenticate

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- read current sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config" -Raw` over key-only
ssh -- raw bytes for curia-side construction; single cmdlet
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_CURRENT»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on read failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (sshd_config missing
or unreadable)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- build new sshd_config content (curia-side)*:
.. strip CR bytes from `«SSHD_CONFIG_CURRENT»` and walk lines: for
each hardening directive (`PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`), replace the
first matching line (commented or otherwise) with the canonical
value and drop subsequent duplicates; append any absent directive
.. strip any prior `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block (lines
from that header to the next `Match ` line or EOF), then append the
canonical block:
+
[listing]
----
Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}
    AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys
----
.. the absolute path decouples sshd's workload authorized_keys
lookup from workload's `$HOME`, so a future profile demotion cannot
lock out auth; parallel to the built-in `Match Group administrators`
block routing to `administrators_authorized_keys`
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_NEW»` (curia bytes)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- write new sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
base64-encode `«SSHD_CONFIG_NEW»` on curia, then over key-only ssh
run `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllBytes("$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config",
[System.Convert]::FromBase64String('<b64>'))` -- single-statement PS
body. Base64 transport avoids in-flight encoding drift across the
cmd.exe argv layer
.. {busc_fatal}
on WriteAllBytes failure -- restart would still serve unhardened auth

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- provision workload authkeys parent directory (CDD)*:
.. {busc_call}
`Test-Path "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"`
over key-only ssh
.. {busc_store}
`«WORKLOAD_AUTHKEYS_DIR_PRESENT»` = `True` or `False`
.. On `«WORKLOAD_AUTHKEYS_DIR_PRESENT»` equal to `False`, {busc_call}
`New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path
"$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}" -Force |
Out-Null` over key-only ssh -- single cmdlet. The directory persists
across {busn_garrison} destroy-and-recreate cycles -- garrison
rewrites the file contents but never touches the directory
.. {busc_fatal}
on probe or create failure -- workload auth path would not resolve

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- lock down workload authkeys parent directory ACL*:
.. {busc_call}
`icacls "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
/inheritance:r /grant 'NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM:F' /grant
'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F'` over key-only ssh -- admins/SYSTEM Full
Control, inheritance disabled, no workload grant; sshd reads as
SYSTEM, parallel to administrators_authorized_keys discipline;
idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `icacls` failure -- sshd would refuse the workload trust file

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- validate sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`& "C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe" -t` over key-only ssh
-- single-statement PS body; penultimate atomic op of phase 1
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- re-read sshd_config (post-write)*:
.. {busc_call}
`Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config" -Raw` over key-only
ssh
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_VERIFY»`
.. {busc_fatal}
on read failure -- post-write state unverifiable

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- verify directives and Match block (curia-side)*:
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_VERIFY»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
directive values (BCG-compliant matching) AND contains a `Match User
${BUJB_workload_user}` block whose `AuthorizedKeysFile` resolves to
`__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys`
.. {busc_fatal}
on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 -- restart sshd*:
.. {busc_call}
`Restart-Service sshd` over key-only ssh -- final atomic op of
phase 1; admin pubkey is installed by step 3 and authkeys ACL
locked down by step 4, so key auth is available for the
restart-issuing call itself
.. {busc_fatal}
on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
expected phase boundary, not a failure)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 -- reconnect + re-verify served sshd_config*:
.. {busc_call}
`Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config" -Raw` over key-only
ssh -- the successful reconnect IS the proof of key-auth restoration
after restart; the Get-Content body satisfies WSG WSp-105 with a
single cmdlet
.. {busc_store}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_SERVED»`
.. {busc_require}
`«SSHD_CONFIG_SERVED»` parses to the three hardening directives AND
the canonical `Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}` block (BCG-compliant
matching; same shape as step 12)
.. {busc_fatal}
on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
session); recover via operator's local console. On directive or
Match-block mismatch -- post-restart drift between pre-restart write
and on-disk state; fast-fail forensic before phase 3's multi-minute
WSL stage burns

//axhos_step
. *Preflight -- verify operator-precondition facts before phase 3*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} operator-precondition fact subset
(`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` registry, `PlatformAoAcOverride`
registry, Tailscale service registered) over key-only ssh
.. {busc_store}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«PREFLIGHT_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope; preflight fails fast
before the multi-minute WSL stage so missing operator posture does
not waste a download cycle

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- provision C:\\bujb-wsl directory*:
.. {busc_call}
`New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'C:\\bujb-wsl' -Force` over
key-only ssh -- idempotent. Holds admin's `rbtww-main` distribution
VHD tree AND serves as the admin↔workload handoff point for the
seed tarball that {busn_garrison} (WSL letter) writes during step
3. Default ACL inheritance from `C:\\` grants
`BUILTIN\\Users:ReadAndExecute`, which is what allows the workload
SSH session in garrison-w step 11 to read the seed -- the seed must
NOT live under workload's Windows profile because admin's
`wsl --export` writes file ACLs that exclude the workload user
.. {busc_fatal}
on directory-creation failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
.. {busc_call}
`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over key-only ssh to
register `rbtww-main` under the admin user at
`C:\\bujb-wsl\\rbtww-main` (canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in
{bujb_jurisdiction})
.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
.. {busc_fatal}
on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
.. {busc_call}
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
`powercfg /hibernate off` over key-only ssh; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 -- set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
.. {busc_call}
`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over key-only ssh; idempotent
.. {busc_fatal}
on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)

//axhos_step
. *Post-completion -- verify caparison deliverables*:
.. {busc_call}
{bust_invigilate_windows} caparison-deliverable fact subset
(Tailscale service `Automatic`, standby-timeout policy = 0 AC+DC,
hibernate disabled, `rbtww-main` WSL distribution registered) over
key-only ssh. Operator-precondition facts already verified at
preflight do not re-run, and admin SSH is not re-probed because
phase 2 just established it
.. {busc_store}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»`
.. {busc_require}
`«DELIVERABLES_RESULT»` is zero
.. {busc_fatal}
on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim

//axhog_guarantee
On success:

* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`, AND a

* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user

* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
* {bust_invigilate_windows}'s caparison-deliverable fact subset
